data.text: Using the `ddx` workflow for this bead because the task is explicitly an execute-bead run and the acceptance criteria are tracker-bound. First I’m reading the implicated agent and reconcile code, then I’ll decide whether this is a direct fix or if the bead must be decomposed.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go && printf '\\n---FILE---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go && printf '\\n---FILE---\\n' && sed -n '1,240p' cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go"

/bin/zsh -lc "pwd && git status --short && rg -n \"FailureModeNoViableProvider|needs-investigation|no viable provider|triage:needs-investigation|execution_failed|no_changes\" cli/internal/agent cli/internal/bead -g '"'!**/vendor/**'"'"

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n           \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\\\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\\\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\\n                    \\\"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\\\"\\n url(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n            \u0026self.url\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    struct TestStore {\\n        adapter: PostgresStorageAdapter,\\n        _database: TestDatabase,\\n }\\n\\n    impl DerefMut for TestStore {\\n        fn deref_mut(\u0026mut self) -\u003e \u0026mut Self::Target {\\n            \u0026mut self.adapter\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    fn postgres_test_guard() -\u003e MutexGuard\u003c'static, ()\u003e {\\n        static GUARD: OnceLock\u003cMutex\u003c()\u003e\u003e = OnceLock::new();\\n        GUARD\\n            .get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))\\n            .lock()\\n            .expect(\\\"PostgreSQL test guard lock should not be poisoned\\\")\\n    }\\n\\n    fn skip_postgres_test(test_name: \u0026str, reason: \u0026str) {\\n        tracing::warn!(test = test_name, reason, \\\"skipping PostgreSQL storage test\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    fn database_or_skip(test_name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003cTestDatabase\u003e {\\n JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n    let auth_str = auth_header\\n        .to_str()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n    auth_str\\n        .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n        (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n            if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n        {\\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n            if database.is_empty() {\\n                None\\n            } else {\\n                Some((tenant, database))\\n            }\\n        }\\n        _ =\u003e None,\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n    fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n        let body = json!({\\n            \\\"error\\\": {\\n                \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n                \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n            }\\n        });\\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. data.text: The core shape is smaller than it looked: the reconcile path already has a stale-label cleanup branch for no-viable-provider, so this is likely a loop-policy fix plus tests rather than a broad tracker change. I’m reading the exact outcome-handling branch and the existing transport/retry helpers next so I can keep the change bounded.

data.text: I found the policy seam: no-viable-provider already becomes a transport cooldown in the loop, while the reconcile package already knows how to scrub stale `triage:needs-investigation`. The missing piece is likely proving the loop never adds that label in the first place and that genuine `status: needs_investigation` remains unchanged.

data.text: The repository already has the production behavior I’d want for the transport path, so the remaining gap is mostly proof: the exact regression tests named in the bead are missing or named differently. I’m verifying the helper constants and then I’ll patch the tests to align with the acceptance contract instead of inventing new logic.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T234939-1782c771/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T234939-1782c771/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T234939-1782c771/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T234939-1782c771/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T234939-1782c771/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-9f20a4bd">     <title>agent: move claim heartbeat out of beads.jsonl</title>     <description> PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently refreshes claimed-bead liveness by rewriting `.ddx/beads.jsonl` every heartbeat interval. In this repo that dirties the git worktree continuously, creates tracker-only commit noise, increases merge conflict risk on the hottest tracker file, and mixes ephemeral worker liveness with durable bead state. FEAT-006 also states the worker/server boundary has no claim lease or heartbeat, so durable tracker heartbeats conflict with the intended execution contract.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:817-832 starts a heartbeat goroutine for each claimed candidate and calls `w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)` on every ticker interval.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:666-687 implements `Store.Heartbeat` by writing `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` and `updated_at` back into `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
- cli/internal/config/types.go:160-164 and 198-206 expose a 30s default worker heartbeat interval, making the tracker rewrite periodic during normal operation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Stop writing periodic liveness heartbeats to `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. Durable tracker mutations should remain limited to semantic transitions such as claim, unclaim, close, cooldown, and append-only evidence/events.
- Replace `Store.Heartbeat` usage in the work loop with an ephemeral liveness surface that does not dirty the tracker. Acceptable implementations include an ignored runtime lease file under `.ddx/workers/` or `.ddx/runs/`, server worker state, or another non-tracker runtime store.
- Preserve stale-claim/watchdog behavior by teaching any stale-worker logic to read the new ephemeral liveness surface or, if no reliable runtime heartbeat exists, to rely only on explicit claim metadata plus process/server lifecycle cleanup.
- Update C11/heartbeat extraction expectations if necessary so it extracts or removes the new safe liveness path rather than preserving tracker writes.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing bead claim semantics (`status=in_progress`, owner, claimed-at, claimed-pid).
- Removing append-only execution evidence or semantic bead events.
- Implementing the Axon bead backend; this bug applies to the JSONL tracker path.

DEPS
- Depends on ddx-06eb05d8 because C7 is currently changing the same drain-loop area.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-0b8780db (C11 heartbeat extraction) should depend on this bead so the extraction does not preserve tracker-writing heartbeat behavior.
- ddx-5cb6e6cd execution refactor should not close while periodic `.ddx/beads.jsonl` liveness writes remain in the work loop.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestWorkLoop_ClaimLivenessDoesNotMutateBeadsJSONL` verifies a running claimed-bead liveness tick does not change `.ddx/beads.jsonl` bytes or the bead `updated_at` field.
2. `TestWorkLoop_LivenessRecordedOutsideTracker` verifies the replacement liveness surface is written/read outside `.ddx/beads.jsonl` and is discoverable by worker/watchdog code.
3. `TestStoreHeartbeat_RemovedOrNoTrackerWrite` verifies `Store.Heartbeat` is removed from the work-loop call path or becomes a non-mutating/deprecated no-op with no tracker write.
4. WIRED-IN: `git grep "Store.Heartbeat|.Heartbeat(candidate.ID)|.Heartbeat(beadID)" cli/internal/agent cli/internal/server` shows no periodic worker liveness path writing through the bead JSONL store.
5. C11 (`ddx-0b8780db`) either depends on this bead or its AC is updated to extract the safe liveness path, not the existing tracker-writing heartbeat.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./internal/server/... -run "Heartbeat|Liveness|Watchdog|Claim" -count=1 passes.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

A transient route/provider outage parked 23 open beads behind `triage:needs-investigation`. Their latest actionable event was `execute-bead summary=execution_failed` with detail/body `execute-loop: all tiers exhausted — no viable provider found`. Once routing recovered, these beads remained excluded from `ddx work` until an operator manually removed the triage label.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:103-108 already classifies `no viable harness`, `no harness configured`, `failed to initialize routing service`, and `no viable provider` as FailureModeNoViableProvider.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:496-499 and cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry.go:137-142 explicitly exclude `no viable provider` from rate-limit retry handling, so this path does not get the bounded HTTP-429 retry treatment.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1191-1195 runs post-attempt triage for non-preserved execution failures and can then suppress progress with cooldown/triage metadata. In the observed queue, that produced durable `triage:needs-investigation` labels for transient provider outages.

PROPOSED FIX
Handle FailureModeNoViableProvider as a retryable transport outage, not a needs-investigation condition. A no-viable-provider attempt should unclaim the bead and either set a short retry cooldown or leave it retryable according to the existing retry policy, but it must not add or preserve `triage:needs-investigation` solely because routing was unavailable. `ddx work` diagnostics should distinguish provider outage from human-investigation blockers.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go
- cli/internal/bead/reconcile.go and reconcile tests if stale labels need cleanup semantics

NON-SCOPE
- Do not treat quota exhaustion as HTTP 429 rate-limit retry; keep the current rate-limit contract boundary.
- Do not remove `triage:needs-investigation` for genuine `status: needs_investigation` no_changes rationales.
- Do not change Fizeau routing or model selection.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestExecuteBeadWorker_NoViableProviderDoesNotAddNeedsInvestigation verifies an execution_failed report classified as FailureModeNoViableProvider leaves the bead open/retryable without `triage:needs-investigation`.
2. TestExecuteBeadWorker_NoViableProviderUsesRetryableTransportPolicy verifies the bead is unclaimed and either has the intended short retry cooldown or is immediately retryable per the chosen policy; it must not be parked as human investigation.
3. TestReconcile_NoViableProviderClearsStaleNeedsInvestigation verifies a bead whose latest event is no-viable-provider has stale `triage:needs-investigation` removed by reconcile/cleanup if that path owns cleanup.
4. TestExecuteBeadWorker_NeedsInvestigationRationaleStillParks verifies genuine `status: needs_investigation` no_changes behavior is unchanged.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorker_NoViableProvider|TestReconcile_NoViableProvider|TestExecuteBeadWorker_NeedsInvestigationRationaleStillParks" -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:routing, area:beads, kind:bug, reliability, prevention, spec:FEAT-010</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-f3dfde0d" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="6e534706a15fb81596a7488710732a98ff73e169" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T235935-b3a79f34"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>   </governing> </execute-bead>

Historical execution events are retained; queue eligibility should now be governed by status and dependencies.     </notes>     <labels>area:routing, area:service, kind:task</labels>   </bead>    <governing>     <ref id="FEAT-004" path="benchmark-results/beadbench/run-20260423T021643Z/helix-build-selector-readiness__codex-gpt54__r1/verify-worktree/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-plugin-packaging.md" title="Feature Specification: FEAT-004 - Plugin Packaging">       <content> <untrusted-data> --- dun:   id: FEAT-004   depends_on:     - helix.prd     - FEAT-002 --- # Feature Specification: FEAT-004 - Plugin Packaging  **Feature ID**: FEAT-004 **Status**: Draft **Priority**: P1 **Owner**: HELIX maintainers  ## Overview  HELIX is currently installed via `scripts/install-local-skills.sh`, which creates symlinks from `~/.claude/skills/` and `~/.agents/skills/` back into the repo checkout. This works but has operational problems: new skills are invisible until the installer is re-run, the symlinks are absolute paths tied to a single checkout location, and the approach bypasses Claude Code's native plugin discovery entirely.  This feature makes HELIX a proper Claude Code plugin so that skills, the CLI, shared workflow resources, and hooks are discovered automatically through the plugin manifest — no manual installer step required.  ## Problem Statement  - **Current situation**: `install-local-skills.sh` symlinks each skill into   `~/.claude/skills/` and `~/.agents/skills/`, and installs a CLI launcher   into `~/.local/bin/helix`. Adding a new skill (e.g., `helix-worker`) has no   effect until the installer is re-run. Users hit "Unknown skill" errors with   no indication that a reinstall is needed. - **Pain points**:   1. Silent skill discovery failures — new skills exist on disk but are not      registered.   2. Absolute symlinks break when the repo moves or is checked out elsewhere.   3. No versioning, no manifest, no way for Claude Code to reason about HELIX      as a coherent package.   4. `bin/helix` is installed via a side-channel (`~/.local/bin`) rather than      through the plugin `bin/` PATH injection.   5. Enterprise and multi-repo deployments have no standard distribution path. - **Desired outcome**: `claude --plugin-dir ./path-to-helix` (or a plugin   install) makes all HELIX skills, the CLI, and shared resources available   immediately — no symlinks, no re-running installers, no absolute paths.  ## Design  ### Plugin layout  The HELIX repo root *is* the plugin root. No separate build or copy step:  ``` helix/                              # plugin root ├── .claude-plugin/ │   └── plugin.json                 # manifest ├── skills/                         # 17+ skills, auto-discovered │   ├── helix-run/ │   │   └── SKILL.md │   ├── helix-worker/ │   │   └── SKILL.md │   └── ... ├── workflows/                      # shared resource library │   ├── actions/ │   ├── EXECUTION.md │   ├── ratchets.md │   └── ... ├── bin/                            # added to Bash PATH by plugin loader │   └── helix                       # CLI entrypoint (thin wrapper) ├── scripts/                        # implementation scripts │   ├── helix                       # actual CLI │   ├── tracker.sh │   └── install-local-skills.sh     # legacy, dev convenience only ├── settings.json                   # plugin default settings (optional) └── hooks/     └── hooks.json                  # plugin hooks (optional) ```  ### Manifest (`plugin.json`)  ```json {   "name": "helix",   "version": "0.1.0",   "description": "HELIX development control system — supervisory autopilot for AI-assisted software delivery",   "author": {     "name": "Erik LaBianca",     "url": "https://github.com/easel"   },   "repository": "https://github.com/easel/helix",   "license": "MIT",   "skills": "./skills/",   "hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json" } ```  Key decisions: - `skills` points to the existing `skills/` directory — no move needed. - `bin/helix` is a thin wrapper that invokes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/helix`.   The plugin loader adds `bin/` to `PATH` automatically. - Skills reference shared resources via `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/workflows/`. - No `commands/` directory — HELIX uses skills exclusively.  ### Resource resolution  Skills currently reference `workflows/` via relative paths that assume the repo root is reachable from the skill directory. The plugin model formalizes this:  - **Inside skill prompts**: Use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/workflows/` for   references that must resolve at runtime. - **Inside the CLI**: `$HELIX_ROOT` (already used) resolves to the plugin root   when invoked via the plugin `bin/` path. - **Validation**: The skill package validator (`tests/validate-skills.sh`) must   verify that every `workflows/` reference in a SKILL.md can resolve from the   plugin root.  ### Relationship to `install-local-skills.sh`  The installer becomes a **development convenience** for contributors who want HELIX skills available outside of `--plugin-dir` mode (e.g., when working in other repos). It is no longer the primary installation path.  The installer should: 1. Detect whether the plugin manifest exists. 2. Print a recommendation to use `--plugin-dir` or `plugin install` instead. 3. Continue with symlink installation for users who explicitly want it.  ### Installation modes  | Mode | Mechanism | Use case | |------|-----------|----------| | Plugin (local) | `claude --plugin-dir /path/to/helix` | Primary — development and daily use | | Plugin (installed) | `claude plugin install helix` | Distribution — when marketplace is available | | Plugin (project) | `.claude/settings.json` with plugin reference | Team — checked into adopting repos | | Symlink (legacy) | `scripts/install-local-skills.sh` | Development convenience only |  ### Skill namespacing  When installed as a plugin, skills are namespaced: `/helix:helix-run`, `/helix:helix-worker`, etc. The unqualified names (`/helix-run`) continue to work when no other plugin provides a conflicting skill name.  ## Requirements  ### Functional Requirements  1. The HELIX repo must contain a `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` manifest that    declares HELIX as a Claude Code plugin. 2. `bin/helix` must be a thin wrapper that resolves `HELIX_ROOT` to    `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` and delegates to `scripts/helix`. 3. All HELIX skills must be discoverable through the plugin's `skills/`    directory without manual symlink installation. 4. Shared resources in `workflows/` must be accessible from skills via    `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/workflows/`. 5. Adding a new skill (creating `skills/<name>/SKILL.md`) must make it    available immediately in the next Claude Code session — no reinstall step. 6. `install-local-skills.sh` must remain functional as a development    convenience but must not be the documented primary installation path. 7. The skill package validator must verify plugin layout integrity: manifest    exists, skills resolve, shared resources reachable. 8. Plugin hooks (if any) must be declared in `hooks/hooks.json` and loaded    by the plugin system.  ### Non-Functional Requirements  - **Portability**: The plugin must work regardless of the repo's absolute path. - **Zero-copy**: The repo root *is* the plugin root — no build step or file   duplication. - **Backward compatibility**: Existing `install-local-skills.sh` users must   not be broken. The installer continues to work but prints a deprecation   notice. - **Testability**: Plugin layout validation must be deterministic and runnable   in CI.  ## User Stories  ### US-001: Install HELIX as a plugin [FEAT-004] **As a** HELIX operator **I want** to add HELIX to my Claude Code session with `--plugin-dir` **So that** all skills, the CLI, and shared resources are available immediately  **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Given a HELIX checkout, when `claude --plugin-dir /path/to/helix` starts,   then all HELIX skills appear in the skill list. - [ ] Given the plugin is loaded, when the user invokes `/helix-run`, then the   skill executes with access to `workflows/` resources. - [ ] Given the plugin is loaded, when `helix status` is run in Bash, then the   CLI is on PATH and functional.  ### US-002: Add a new skill without reinstalling [FEAT-004] **As a** HELIX maintainer **I want** new skills to be available in the next session after I create the file **So that** I do not need to remember to re-run an installer  **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Given a new `skills/helix-foo/SKILL.md` is created, when a new Claude   Code session starts with the plugin, then `/helix-foo` is available. - [ ] Given the old installer has not been re-run, when the plugin is loaded,   then the new skill is still available.  ### US-003: Team distribution via project settings [FEAT-004] **As a** team lead adopting HELIX for a project **I want** to declare HELIX as a plugin dependency in `.claude/settings.json` **So that** all team members get HELIX skills automatically  **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Given `.claude/settings.json` references the HELIX plugin, when a team   member starts Claude Code in the project, then HELIX skills are available. - [ ] Given the plugin is loaded from project settings, when the team member   invokes `helix run`, then the CLI and shared resources resolve correctly.  ### US-004: Validate plugin layout [FEAT-004] **As a** HELIX maintainer **I want** CI to catch broken plugin layouts before merge **So that** users never get a plugin that silently fails  **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Given `tests/validate-skills.sh` runs, when the plugin manifest is   missing or malformed, then the test fails with a clear error. - [ ] Given a skill references a `workflows/` resource that does not exist,   when the validator runs, then it reports the broken reference.  ## Edge Cases and Error Handling  - **Plugin loaded without `workflows/`**: Skills must fail clearly with   "HELIX shared resources not found at ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/workflows/"   rather than silently producing degraded output. - **Both plugin and symlink install active**: Plugin takes precedence (Claude   Code's plugin skills override user-level skills of the same name). The   symlink install becomes a no-op when the plugin is active. - **Repo moved after symlink install**: Symlinks break (existing behavior).   Plugin mode is unaffected since it uses the current plugin root. - **Multiple HELIX versions**: If two plugin dirs both provide `helix-*`   skills, Claude Code's plugin precedence rules apply. Users should not load   two HELIX plugins simultaneously.  ## Success Metrics  - HELIX is loadable as a Claude Code plugin with zero manual setup beyond   `--plugin-dir`. - New skills are available in the next session without any installer step. - `install-local-skills.sh` is no longer referenced in primary documentation   as the installation path. - Plugin layout validation runs in CI and catches broken layouts.  ## Constraints and Assumptions  - The HELIX repo root doubles as the plugin root — no separate packaging step. - Claude Code's plugin loader must support `bin/` PATH injection and   `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` variable resolution in skill prompts. - The plugin manifest format follows the Claude Code plugin specification. - Marketplace distribution is a future concern — local `--plugin-dir` is   sufficient for V1.  ## Dependencies  - **FEAT-002**: CLI feature spec (installation section needs updating) - **SD-001**: Solution design (packaging component needs plugin layout) - **helix.prd**: PRD packaging requirements - Claude Code plugin specification (external dependency)  ## Out of Scope  - Marketplace publishing workflow - Plugin auto-update mechanism - Plugin configuration UI - Enterprise managed-settings distribution (future, once marketplace exists)  ## Open Questions  - Does `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` resolve inside SKILL.md reference paths, or   only in hook commands and MCP configs? If not, skills may need a different   mechanism to locate `workflows/`. - Should the plugin declare MCP servers (e.g., for tracker access) or keep   tracker interaction purely CLI-based? - What is the right versioning strategy — semver tied to HELIX releases, or   independent plugin version? </untrusted-data>       </content>     </ref>   </governing>    <diff rev="c56ce079dc5f89c6ad99bb784e0a8a83aa559303"> <untrusted-data> commit c56ce079dc5f89c6ad99bb784e0a8a83aa559303 Merge: 6e86534 d2998f1 Author: ddx-land-coordinator <coordinator@ddx.local> Date:   Wed May 6 20:05:21 2026 -0400      Merge bead fizeau-b4c78d49 attempt 20260506T235641- into master </untrusted-data>   </diff>    <instructions> You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria. That distinction matters before I decide whether I need to change code or just report the environment block.

data.text: I found a confounder: my separate diagnostic race run was overlapping the commit hook and can perturb git-heavy tests. I’m stopping the extra probing and only checking whether the original commit hook has now advanced on its own.

The repo-wide race run exposed a broader issue: the `internal/agent` package already has failing tests unrelated to this bead, and the hook surfaces them before it can complete. I’m checking whether those failures are driven by a local config/default-model mismatch that I can correct without touching unrelated code paths; if not, I’ll need to report the hook block as pre-existing.

data.text: I’m writing the required DDx rationale now because the bead-specific tests are green but the repository’s pre-commit race suite blocks a clean commit. This keeps the attempt evidence explicit instead of leaving the run undocumented.

No commit was made.

I added the requested regression coverage in [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-66c98cde-20260506T235935-b3a79f34/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go) and verified the bead-specific tests pass, but the repo-wide `lefthook run pre-commit` gate fails in the existing `go test -short -race ./internal/agent` step on unrelated tests. I recorded the blocker in [`.ddx/executions/20260506T235935-b3a79f34/no_changes_rationale.txt`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-66c98cde-20260506T235935-b3a79f34/.ddx/executions/20260506T235935-b3a79f34/no_changes_rationale.txt) with `status: needs_investigation`.
